title: Universal Launch Report tab with live canister metrics (KD Platform)

requirements.summary: Create an authenticated Universal Launch Report page that matches KD/NOC styling tokens and renders the “KD.WebAI NOC v13.1 - Universal Launch Report” view in English.
acceptanceCriteria: A new report UI is available as a React component/page and visually matches the existing KD/NOC styling (kd-* classes) rather than raw default Tailwind grays.
All user-facing strings on the report page are in English.
The page requires Internet Identity authentication (same gating behavior as KDDashboardPage/NOCPage).

file_operations.description: Create the authenticated “KD.WebAI NOC v13.1 - Universal Launch Report” page with KD/NOC Tailwind tokens (e.g., bg-kd-bg, bg-kd-panel, text-kd-text) and English-only UI text. Mirror the existing login-gated pattern used in KDDashboardPage/NOCPage. Use shadcn-ui building blocks already used in the app (e.g., Card, Button, Badge, Alert) to structure the report; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add a TanStack Router route for the Universal Launch Report and a KD Platform dashboard navigation entry to open it.
acceptanceCriteria: A new TanStack Router route exists for the report page (do not use react-router-dom <Route>).
KD Platform Dashboard (frontend/src/pages/KDDashboardPage.tsx) includes a navigation control that routes to the report page.
Navigation works in both desktop and mobile layouts without breaking existing routes (/kd-platform/dashboard, /kd-platform/noc).

file_operations.description: Register a new TanStack Router route (createRoute) for the Universal Launch Report page (e.g., /kd-platform/universal-launch-report) and include it in the routeTree alongside existing KD Platform routes.

Add an in-app navigation control (Button/link) in the KD Platform dashboard flow that routes to the new Universal Launch Report route using TanStack Router navigation patterns. Use existing KD styling tokens and existing shadcn-ui primitives; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Fetch and display live report metrics with React Query + useActor (treasury/TVL, Omni-Mesh, cycles balance, DAO proposals) and poll every 10 seconds with resilient loading/error handling.
acceptanceCriteria: The report page uses React Query (not manual setInterval state management) to load the required metrics via the backend actor.
The report metrics automatically refresh at least every 10 seconds while the page is open.
Loading and error states are handled (e.g., a loading indicator and a non-crashing error message/log) and the page remains usable if one metric fails.
DAO proposals render as a list using the live proposal fields returned by getDaoProposals (id/title/description/votes).

file_operations.description: Add report-specific React Query hooks that compose the existing actor flow (useActor) to query getTreasuryStatus, getOmniMeshStatus, getCyclesBalance, and getDaoProposals with refetchInterval set to 10000ms. Ensure each metric query is independently enabled and fails independently so the page remains usable if one query errors.

Wire the report page to the report-specific hooks (React Query) and render the required sections: treasury status/TVL, Omni-Mesh status, cycles balance, and DAO proposals list (id/title/description/votesFor/votesAgainst). Add per-metric loading and error UI (non-crashing) using existing shadcn-ui primitives where appropriate; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Wire the new getCyclesBalance backend capability into the frontend report and align frontend types to allow calling it via the existing typed actor mechanism.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/main.mo defines a new query method getCyclesBalance that returns the canister’s current cycles balance and enforces user-level access (same AccessControl.hasPermission(..., #user) pattern as getTreasuryStatus/getOmniMeshStatus).
Frontend compiles and can call getCyclesBalance via the typed actor bindings without TypeScript errors.
No state migration (backend/migration.mo) is introduced/required for this change.

file_operations.description: Ensure the report cycles-balance query calls actor.getCyclesBalance() via the same useActor mechanism used elsewhere and returns a bigint suitable for display formatting in the report UI.
Display cycles balance in the Universal Launch Report with an appropriate KD/NOC-styled metric card/section and graceful error handling if unauthorized or unavailable.

requirements.summary: Update frontend canister/type bindings so getCyclesBalance is available to TypeScript and the report can call it without changing immutable hook files.
acceptanceCriteria: TypeScript build succeeds with no missing-method errors for getCyclesBalance.
The report page can call the backend actor method for cycles balance using the same actor creation mechanism already used by existing hooks (do not modify immutable hook files).

file_operations.description: Update/confirm the generated/declared backend interface typing so getCyclesBalance(): Promise<bigint> is present on the typed actor interface used by frontend imports (ensuring TS compilation succeeds).

Import the typed backend interface types (as needed) consistently with existing patterns so actor.getCyclesBalance is type-safe and produces no TypeScript errors, without modifying immutable hook files.
